nodes.name: document:ml/models/dynamic_eval_report.md
observations: Eval report for dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx Transformer model
Dataset: synthetic landmark sequences, 8 classes × 200 examples
Sequence: T=45 frames, feature_dim=126 (63 positions + 63 velocities)
Model: Transformer encoder, d_model=64, 4 heads, 2 layers, 78,600 params
Held-out top-1 accuracy: 0.991 (NFR-9 target ≥ 0.80 — pass)
PyTorch ↔ ONNX prediction parity: 1.000
Model file size: 359.5 KB
Classes: HELLO, GOODBYE, YES, NO, PLEASE, THANKS, SORRY, HELP
Fairness gate NFR-10 pass with 0.9pp worst gap
Served via onnxruntime-web in browser

nodes.name: document:ml/models/eval_report.md
observations: Eval report for fingerspell_mlp.onnx MLP model
Dataset: synthetic landmarks, 24 classes × 400 examples
Model: sklearn MLPClassifier, hidden=(128, 64), early stopping
Collision-aware accuracy: 0.928 (NFR-9 target ≥ 0.85 — pass)
Strict top-1 accuracy: 0.452 (penalises visually-identical-in-synth letters)
Collision-aware vs strict gap: 47.6 pp due to synthetic data limitations
Documents 5 synth collision groups: {A,E,M,N,S,T}, {D,G,L,Q}, {H,U,V}, {K,P}, {C,O}
Fairness gate NFR-10 pass with 0.2pp worst gap
Served via onnxruntime-web in browser

nodes.name: document:BUILD_PROMPT_ML_EXTENSION.md
observations: Build prompt for SignTutor v2 ML extension phase
Hand this file to a coding agent to ship v2 without further input
v1 shipped with signtutor/ directory, pnpm typecheck/test/build all pass
8 architectural decisions preserved from v1
Recognition models: 24-letter sklearn MLP (static, 72 KB) + 8-sign Transformer (dynamic, 360 KB)
Both models trained on synthetic data, served via onnxruntime-web
v2 objectives: real data, J/Z motion letters, 200-sign vocabulary, NMM scoring
On-device personalization, confidence calibration, multi-language, continuous-sign research
8 workstreams defined: W1-W8 with independent shipping
8 new ADRs to document: ADR-002 through ADR-008
Hard requirements preserved from v1 plus 5 new v2 requirements
Definition of done checklist included

nodes.name: document:requirements.txt
observations: SignTutor full stack Python requirements
Core: numpy>=1.24.0
ML: scikit-learn>=1.3.0, torch>=2.0.0,<2.12.0, onnxruntime>=1.16.0
ML: onnx>=1.14.0, skl2onnx>=1.15.0, onnxscript>=0.1.0
CV: opencv-python>=4.8.0, mediapipe>=0.10.0
Frontend is Next.js (signtutor/package.json), not Python
Optional: fastapi, uvicorn for Python API server
Dev optional: pytest, matplotlib

nodes.name: document:signtutor-redesign-prompt.md
observations: Complete redesign build prompt for SignTutor UI/UX
Design direction: Bioluminescent Studio — dark, focused, alive
Stack: Next.js + Tailwind CSS, preserve all functional logic
Comprehensive design tokens: backgrounds, accents, text, borders, typography, shadows
10 page layouts redesigned: Home, About, Learn, Fingerspelling, Letter Practice, Settings, Words, Practice, Setup Wizard
Animation system: page transitions, micro-interactions, reduced motion support
Responsive breakpoints: mobile-first, sm/md/lg/xl
Accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA, focus rings, skip-to-content, ARIA labels
Color is never the only differentiator

nodes.name: dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx
observations: Transformer encoder model for dynamic sign recognition
8 classes: HELLO, GOODBYE, YES, NO, PLEASE, THANKS, SORRY, HELP
Architecture: d_model=64, 4 heads, 2 layers, 78,600 params
Input: T=45 frames, feature_dim=126
Accuracy on synthetic data: 0.991
Model size: 359.5 KB (ONNX)
CPU-trained
Served via onnxruntime-web

nodes.name: fingerspell_mlp.onnx
observations: sklearn MLPClassifier for static fingerspelling recognition
24 letter classes (A-Y, excluding J/Z)
Architecture: hidden layers (128, 64)
Collision-aware accuracy: 0.928 on synthetic data
Strict accuracy: 0.452 due to synthetic data limitations
Model size: 72 KB (ONNX)
Served via onnxruntime-web

nodes.name: SignTutor v2 ML Extension
observations: Build phase: extend SignTutor with real data, more models, and new capabilities
8 workstreams: W1 Real data, W2 J/Z letters, W3 200-sign vocabulary
W4 NMM scoring, W5 On-device personalization, W6 Confidence calibration
W7 Multi-language, W8 Continuous-sign research preview
Preserves all v1 invariants
Requires 8 new ADRs (ADR-002 through ADR-008)

nodes.name: SignTutor UI Redesign
observations: Complete UI/UX redesign of SignTutor application
Bioluminescent Studio dark theme design language
10 pages redesigned with full component specifications
Preserves all ML logic, webcam integration, ONNX loading
Preserves FSRS algorithm, localStorage schema, routing structure
Preserves on-device privacy guarantee and ARIA landmarks

nodes.name: NFR-9 Accuracy Gate
observations: Accuracy target for ML models
Static fingerspelling: ≥ 0.85 collision-aware (v1 target)
Dynamic signs: ≥ 0.80 (v1 target)
v2 targets adjust to real data: static ≥ 0.80, dynamic ≥ 0.65

nodes.name: NFR-10 Fairness Gate
observations: Fairness gate: no slice may be more than 5pp below overall accuracy
v1 uses synthetic placeholder slices (even/odd index)
v2 to add real demographic slices: skin tone (Monk scale), handedness, age bracket, eyewear, lighting, camera angle

nodes.name: synth_collision_groups
observations: Letters visually identical in synthetic data due to simplified finger modeling
5 collision groups: {A,E,M,N,S,T}, {D,G,L,Q}, {H,U,V}, {K,P}, {C,O}
Collision-aware accuracy credits predictions within same equivalence class
Gap between collision-aware (0.928) and strict (0.452) is 47.6 pp
Expected to collapse on real data with finger-curvature and orientation cues

nodes.name: mediapipe_integration
observations: MediaPipe Hands used for hand landmark detection
v2 to use MediaPipe Holistic (hands + pose + face) for NMM scoring
Holistic adds 468 face landmarks and 33 pose landmarks per frame
Outputs normalized landmark sequences for ONNX model input
On-device processing, no video uploads

nodes.name: onnxruntime_web
observations: ONNX Runtime Web loads .onnx models in browser via JavaScript
Used for both fingerspell_mlp.onnx and dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx
CDN loaded from jsdelivr: ort.min.js
InferenceSession.create for model loading
Tensor creation from flattened landmark features
